questions.q: In which language's film industry was Sivaji Ganesan primarily a leading actor?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan was the most acclaimed dramatic actor of Tamil cinema.

questions.q: Which 1952 film marked the debut of Sivaji Ganesan as a film actor?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan made his film debut in Parasakthi, released in 1952.

questions.q: By what honorific title was the acclaimed actor Sivaji Ganesan widely known?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan was popularly honoured with the title 'Nadigar Thilagam' (Pride of Actors).

questions.q: Which prestigious lifetime achievement award did Sivaji Ganesan receive in 1996?
options: National Film Award for Best Actor
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, in 1996.

questions.q: In which year did the actor Sivaji Ganesan pass away?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan died in 2001.

questions.q: Roughly how many films did Sivaji Ganesan act in over his career?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan acted in roughly 300 films across his long career.

questions.q: The screen name 'Sivaji', adopted by the actor Sivaji Ganesan, was inspired by his portrayal of which historical figure on stage?
questions.explanation: He took the name 'Sivaji' after playing the Maratha king Chhatrapati Shivaji in a stage play.

questions.q: In which 1959 historical film did Sivaji Ganesan play the freedom fighter Veerapandiya Kattabomman?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan played the title role in Veerapandiya Kattabomman (1959).

questions.q: Sivaji Ganesan was frequently described as a master of which film craft in particular?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan was renowned as Tamil cinema's most acclaimed dramatic actor.

questions.q: In which decade was the actor Sivaji Ganesan born?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan was born in 1928, in the 1920s.

questions.q: Sivaji Ganesan was the first Indian actor to win a Best Actor award at which international festival in 1960?
questions.explanation: He won Best Actor at the Afro-Asian Film Festival in 1960, a first for an Indian actor at an international festival.

questions.q: In which year was the film Parasakthi, the debut of Sivaji Ganesan, released?
questions.explanation: Parasakthi was released in 1952.

questions.q: The dialogues of Parasakthi (1952), which launched Sivaji Ganesan, were written by which prominent Tamil figure?
questions.explanation: M. Karunanidhi wrote the celebrated dialogues of Parasakthi (1952).

questions.q: In which 1968 film did Sivaji Ganesan play the nagaswaram (wind-instrument) artist Shanmugasundaram?
questions.explanation: In Thillana Mohanambal (1968) Sivaji Ganesan played the nagaswaram artist Shanmugasundaram opposite Padmini.

questions.q: In the acclaimed 1961 film Pasamalar, Sivaji Ganesan portrayed a character sharing a deep bond of which relationship?
questions.explanation: Pasamalar (1961) is celebrated for its emotional brother-sister relationship, with Savitri as his sister.

questions.q: Which actress played Sivaji Ganesan's devoted sister in the 1961 Tamil drama Pasamalar?
questions.explanation: Savitri played Sivaji Ganesan's sister Radha in Pasamalar (1961), one of Tamil cinema's most celebrated brother-sister films.

questions.q: In which 1964 mythological film did Sivaji Ganesan portray the Pandava warrior Karna?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan played the title role of Karna in the epic film Karnan (1964).

questions.q: In the 1965 film Thiruvilaiyadal, Sivaji Ganesan memorably portrayed which deity?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan played Lord Shiva in the mythological classic Thiruvilaiyadal (1965).

questions.q: Sivaji Ganesan was awarded which French honour during his career in recognition of his contribution to art?
options: Chevalier of the Ordre des Arts et des Lettres
questions.explanation: The French government made Sivaji Ganesan a Chevalier of the Ordre des Arts et des Lettres in 1995.

questions.q: Which contemporary Tamil superstar was both a fellow leading man and a noted rival of Sivaji Ganesan during the 1950s and 1960s?
questions.explanation: M. G. Ramachandran (MGR) was Sivaji Ganesan's great contemporary and rival in Tamil cinema.

questions.q: Under which film production banner, named after himself, did Sivaji Ganesan produce several of his own films?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan ran his own banner, Sivaji Productions, which produced several of his films.

questions.q: In the 1985 film Mudhal Mariyathai, the veteran Sivaji Ganesan played a village headman opposite which actress in a critically praised performance?
questions.explanation: In Mudhal Mariyathai (1985) Sivaji Ganesan starred alongside Radha in a widely praised late-career role.

questions.q: What was the birth name of the actor who became famous as Sivaji Ganesan?
questions.explanation: He was born Villupuram Chinniah Ganesan, with 'Sivaji' added after a famous stage role.

questions.q: In which year was the actor Sivaji Ganesan born?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan was born on 1 October 1928.

questions.q: In the 1956 Tamil version of Devadas, which iconic tragic hero was played by Sivaji Ganesan?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan played the title role of the doomed lover Devadas in the 1956 Tamil film.

questions.q: In which 1969 film did Sivaji Ganesan play a celebrated multiple-role performance, demonstrating his versatility?
questions.explanation: In Deiva Magan (1969) Sivaji Ganesan played multiple roles in a performance noted for his versatility.

questions.q: To which house of the Indian Parliament was Sivaji Ganesan nominated in 1982?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan was nominated to the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of India's Parliament, in 1982.

questions.q: Before entering films, Sivaji Ganesan built his early reputation in which performing tradition?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan first made his name as a stage theatre actor before his 1952 film debut.

questions.q: In which Indian state did Sivaji Ganesan spend the bulk of his film career, centred on its film industry?
questions.explanation: Sivaji Ganesan worked chiefly in the Tamil film industry based in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Sivaji Ganesan's son Prabhu, who also became a Tamil actor, made his lead debut in which 1981 film?
questions.explanation: Prabhu, son of Sivaji Ganesan, made his lead debut in Sankarlal (1981).
